description: Scenario catalogue for cross-module behaviours that combine parser, template engine and CLI parsing concerns.

scenarios.objective: Verify that valid sample BibTeX input is parsed into expected entry metadata.
expected_outcomes: Parsing succeeds without errors.
At least one entry is returned.
Representative entry keys and entry types match fixture expectations.

scenarios.objective: Verify that author names in both supported formats are normalised and exposed as structured parts.
expected_outcomes: Normalised author display values use a consistent representation.
Structured given-name and family-name parts are available for template rendering.

scenarios.objective: Verify that author list splitting and name-part semantics follow BibTeX conventions for brace-protected literal names, case-insensitive separators, line-wrapped author fields, nobiliary particles and suffixes.
expected_outcomes: Brace-protected corporate names are treated as single literal authors and are never split at an embedded 'and'.
The author list separator is matched case-insensitively and across line-wrapped whitespace.
Nobiliary particles are exposed as part of the family-name part and suffixes are preserved in the normalised full name.

scenarios.objective: Verify that line-wrapped BibTeX field values are flattened without gaining extra internal whitespace from source indentation.
expected_outcomes: Whitespace runs containing a line break collapse to a single space in template-visible field values.
Line-wrapped title and keyword values match their single-line equivalents.

scenarios.objective: Verify that non-standard BibTeX fields are preserved and exposed to templates.
expected_outcomes: Non-standard fields remain available through the template context.
Rendered output includes representative non-standard values.

scenarios.objective: Verify that month and day values are normalised to zero-prefixed numeric strings.
expected_outcomes: Named and numeric month values are converted to a consistent numeric representation.
Single-digit month and day values are zero-prefixed.

scenarios.objective: Verify that keywords are exposed as a slugified string array for template consumption.
expected_outcomes: The slugified keywords field is available as an array.
Non-alphanumeric characters are replaced with hyphens in each slugified keyword.

scenarios.objective: Verify that the original BibTeX entry is exposed through the raw_bibtex field.
expected_outcomes: The raw_bibtex field is present in the template context.
Rendered output preserves representative original BibTeX content.

scenarios.objective: Verify that single-output rendering exposes the entries collection and supports deterministic output naming inputs.
expected_outcomes: The template context exposes the entries collection.
Rendered output can iterate across all selected entries.
Inputs needed for combined single-mode file naming are available and testable.

scenarios.objective: Verify that include-type and exclude-type selectors apply correctly and combine predictably with key selectors.
expected_outcomes: Include-type returns only entries with matching entry_type values.
Exclude-type omits entries with excluded entry_type values.
When key and type selectors are both provided, both constraints are applied.

scenarios.objective: Verify that invalid template or input conditions surface actionable errors rather than being silently ignored.
expected_outcomes: Invalid template syntax produces an error that exposes the underlying rendering or parsing issue.
Missing or invalid input artefacts fail immediately with an informative error message.

scenarios.objective: Verify that latex_substitute does not modify content inside supported math mode regions while still substituting outside those regions.
expected_outcomes: Substitutions are applied for matching LaTeX snippets outside math mode regions.
Content enclosed by $...$, $$...$$, \(...\), and \[...\] remains unchanged.

scenarios.objective: Verify that latex_substitute preserves supported math mode regions when real built-in default-map tokens are present.
expected_outcomes: Built-in default substitutions are applied for matching LaTeX snippets outside math mode regions.
Real built-in tokens inside $...$, $$...$$, \(...\), and \[...\] remain unchanged.

scenarios.objective: Verify that latex_substitute treats an unclosed $$ opener as plain text and still preserves a later valid $...$ math segment.
expected_outcomes: The unmatched $$ opener is treated as plain text for substitution purposes.
A later well-formed $...$ segment remains unchanged.
Substitutions continue to apply outside preserved math segments.

scenarios.objective: Verify that latex_substitute does not re-process replacement output as fresh substitution input within the same pass.
expected_outcomes: A replacement emitted by one substitution key is not re-matched by later keys in the same substitution pass.
Output reflects single-pass matching over source text regions only.

scenarios.objective: Verify that latex_substitute honours the LaTeX command-token boundary rule so that control-word substitution keys do not corrupt longer commands sharing the same prefix.
expected_outcomes: A control-word key such as \L does not match inside a longer command such as \LaTeX, which is left unsubstituted.
A control-word key immediately followed by a non-letter character is still substituted.
A control-symbol key with a trailing argument letter, such as \"o, still matches when followed by further letters.

scenarios.objective: Verify that template engine default initialisation does not silently degrade and still exposes required helper functionality.
expected_outcomes: When default initialisation succeeds, latex_substitute helper function and filter are available to templates.
If default initialisation fails, construction fails immediately with an informative error rather than silently falling back to an unconfigured engine.

scenarios.objective: Verify that math mode region consistency requirements are enforced: if math segment counts diverge between parser and raw-text detection, the system preserves content rather than silently dropping it.
expected_outcomes: Math regions in standard formats ($...$, $$...$$, \(...\), \[...\]) are consistently identified.
If internal math segment count mismatch is detected, the system falls back to safe content rather than silently dropping math regions.
Normal cases without mismatch continue to preserve original math content without diagnostic warnings.

scenarios.objective: Verify that directory parsing rejects .bib files that resolve outside the scanned directory, for example through symbolic links.
expected_outcomes: Directory parsing fails with an error identifying that the file resolves outside the permitted directory.
No entry content sourced from outside the scanned directory is returned.
